newsitems.title: World Update 21: Australia
newsitems.author: Jayneivana
newsitems.contents: [p][/p][previewyoutube="GqcqtdpavV0;full"][/previewyoutube][p][i]Available today, World Update 21: Australia sets a new standard in realism for pilots in the Microsoft Flight Simulator world![/i][/p][p]Australia is home to some of the world’s most spectacular sights and destinations, including stunning coastlines, vast deserts, beautiful mountains, and architectural marvels. Renowned attractions include the Sydney Opera House, one of the most distinctive structures in the world; the Twelve Apostles, iconic sea stacks on the Southern Ocean coastline; vast sweeps of interior desert; and lush stands of rainforest. World Update 21 delivers Australia to Microsoft Flight Simulator aviators like never before and includes a collection of missions for pilots to explore some of the finest sights that this wonderful country offers.[/p][p]The [i]Microsoft Flight Simulator[/i] team partnered with Bing Maps, Vexcel, Gaya and Asobo to create World Update 21, the first update to Australia in the [i]Microsoft Flight Simulator[/i] world since we released World Update 7 in January of 2021. An immense update that sets new standards in lifelike realism and visual fidelity, the release includes 40 areas of interest (AOIs) recreated using updated photogrammetry and TIN (triangulated irregular network) surface texturing. Coverage of AOIs spans all six Australian states and its two main self-governing territories. Areas of interest throughout Australia’s regions covered in this update include:[/p][h3][b]New South Wales[/b][/h3][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/fc34c1961621def652034de90571728aa322c993.png"][/img][/p][list][*][p]Albury: Located on the Bank of the Murray River, a popular tourist metropolitan area.[/p][/*][*][p]Blue Mountains: Known for sandstone cliffs, deep gorges, and iconic geologic formations.[/p][/*][*][p]Central Coast: Region of several national parks, beaches, and several picturesque towns.[/p][/*][*][p]Chatswood: Bustling commercial suburb in Sydney’s Lower North Shore.[/p][/*][*][p]Coffs Harbour: Locally called Coffs, this city is where lush mountains meet beautiful beaches of the Mid North Coast.[/p][/*][*][p]Minto: A modern suburb of southwestern Sydney.[/p][/*][*][p]Newcastle-Maitland: Located adjacent to one another north of Sydney, Newcastle is known for its beaches and Maitland is a gateway to renowned Hunter Valley vineyards.[/p][/*][*][p]Springwood: Nestled in the Blue Mountains, a historic town that serves as a gateway to several regional natural areas.[/p][/*][*][p]Sydney: The capital of New South Wales and Australia’s most populous city, it is widely heralded as one of the world’s most sophisticated metropolitan areas.[/p][/*][*][p]Wagga Wagga: Australia’s most populous inland city, known for its beautiful botanic gardens and rich Aboriginal history.[/p][/*][*][p]Wollongong: Internationally famous for its dramatic coastal setting and surfing beaches.[/p][/*][/list][h3][b]Queensland[/b][/h3][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/d59b215e1f866b9832d726ef7d90563c49444af6.png"][/img][/p][list][*][p]Brisbane: The capital of Queensland, a modern, outdoor-oriented city hosting the 2032 Olympics.[/p][/*][*][p]Bundaberg: Located on the southern Queensland coast, it is one of the major gateways to the Great Barrier Reef and an important sugar cane hub.[/p][/*][*][p]Cairns: Located in coastal North Queensland, it is also one of the key entry points to the Great Barrier Reef and Wet Tropics rainforest.[/p][/*][*][p]Gold Coast: Coastal region known for surfside skyscrapers and expansive beaches.[/p][/*][*][p]Hervey Bay: Located on the southern Queensland coast, one of Australia’s premier whale watching destinations.[/p][/*][*][p]Kippa-Ring: A renowned commercial suburb in the city of Moreton Bay on the Redcliffe Peninsula along Queensland’s southern coast.[/p][/*][*][p]Mackay: Called the “sugar capital” of Australia, it is a beautiful port city on Queensland’s central coast and serves the sugar cane processing and mining industries.[/p][/*][*][p]Sunshine Coast: One of Australia’s premier travel destinations due to its white-sand beaches, surfing, and the Australia Zoo.[/p][/*][*][p]Toowoomba: Australia’s second most populous inland city, known as the “Garden City” due to its more than 150 parks.[/p][/*][*][p]Townsville: Located on the northeastern coast of Queensland adjacent to the central portion of the Great Barrier Reef; a major industrial hub.[/p][/*][/list][h3][b]South Australia[/b][/h3][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/8e9a32525378f4030290973512b14544ea5ab850.jpg"][/img][/p][list][*][p]Adelaide: The coastal capital of South Australia, a planned city surrounded by lush parks.[/p][/*][*][p]Woodcroft: Picturesque suburb of southern Adelaide.[/p][/*][/list][h3][b]Tasmania[/b][/h3][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/d1443610a3d134cbeb77aa045097e9597071af44.jpg"][/img][/p][list][*][p]Hobart: The capital of and largest city in Tasmania known for its dramatic natural setting and colonial buildings.[/p][/*][*][p]Launceston: A historic city in northern Tasmania known for its 19th-century architecture.[/p][/*][/list][h3][b]Victoria[/b][/h3][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/61703efc9c8028490963ba667b9a1fbd4fa0d111.png"][/img][/p][list][*][p]Melbourne: The capital of Victoria, and Australia’s center of culture and sports.[/p][/*][*][p]Geelong: A major port city known for its rich history, the eastern anchor of the famous Great Ocean Road.[/p][/*][*][p]Bendigo: A town known for its 1850s gold rush history and Victorian-era architecture.[/p][/*][*][p]Frankston: Located on the eastern coast of Port Phillip, southeast of Melbourne, known for its beaches and boardwalk.[/p][/*][*][p]Mildura: Located in northwestern Victoria along the border with New South Wales, a major producer of grapes, citrus, and dried fruits.[/p][/*][*][p]Moorabbin: A key suburb in the greater Melbourne metropolitan area, a renowned aviation destination, notably due to the Moorabbin Air Museum.[/p][/*][*][p]Mooroolbark: a key suburb of Outer Eastern Melbourne known for its Aboriginal history.[/p][/*][*][p]Shepparton: This diverse city in northern Victoria is known for its agricultural vibrancy, notably its fruit processing industry.[/p][/*][/list][p] [/p][h3][b]Western Australia[/b][/h3][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/e169aa91fa2a4413e183abf1f5162eebc3a1bf67.png"][/img][/p][list][*][p]Perth: The capital of Western Australia known for its beautiful beaches, surrounding desert national parks, and its relative isolation.[/p][/*][*][p]Jandakot: A southern suburb of Perth, a major aviation hub due to its busy general aviation airport, Jandakot Airport (YPJT), and several aviation-related industries like flight training and air tourism.[/p][/*][*][p]Joondalup: Located just inland of the Indian Ocean, it is a major northern suburb of Perth that is often called the “city of the north.”[/p][/*][*][p]Mandurah: A vibrant coastal city south of Perth known for its large estuary with abundant bird and maritime wildlife.[/p][/*][*][p]Bunbury: A coastal city south of Perth, called the “city of dolphins” and known for its beaches and marine life.[/p][/*][/list][h3][b]Australian Capital Territory[/b][/h3][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/5421d797cf6ed76d17016e4fc462cd924ac9fa99.png"][/img][/p][list][*][p]Canberra: The national capital of Australia, it is one of the world’s best known planned cities and features stunning architecture and several renowned urban parks.[/p][/*][/list][h3][b]Northern Territory[/b][/h3][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/001ff345a6e38afecf518db36f2ef1eaf58e5d17.png"][/img][/p][list][*][p]Darwin: The capital and most populous city of Australia’s Northern Territory known as the center of the Northern Territory’s extensive natural attractions, including coastal and desert destinations.[/p][/*][/list][p][i]Microsoft Flight Simulator[/i]: World Update 21 also includes dozens of points of interest (POIs), including parks, dams, lighthouses, observatories, and major buildings.[/p][p]The update comprises six meticulously handcrafted airports from Microsoft Flight Simulator’s long-term partner, Gaya:[/p][p][b]Broken Hill Airport (YBHI):[/b] Located deep in the outback desert of far-western New South Wales, it is a key regional hub for the area and an important base for the Royal Flying Doctor Service, which services healthcare to Australia’s rural and remote regions.[/p][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/2d21d79c8cb35d6b64fc6bcb79345cbddddf4ab6.png"][/img][/p][p][b]Broome International Airport (YBRM):[/b] Located on the Indian Ocean coast of the northwestern part of Western Australia, this airport is known as the Gateway to the Kimberley, a renowned desert and coastal wilderness area.[/p][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/5d762c859594341e4a230992e55c04e94078733b.png"][/img][/p][p][b]Coober Pedy Airport (YCBP):[/b] Located amid the world’s premier opal mining region in the outback of South Australia, this airport is a small facility with a paved runway and a gravel runway serving the town of Coober Pedy and surroundings.[/p][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/933ddfd3b9b05fcb5656191b59e618c6cea815ba.png"][/img][/p][p][b]Bathurst Airport (YBTH):[/b] A small airport located outside the New South Wales town of Bathurst in Australia’s Central Tablelands region, an area known for its beautiful low mountains.[/p][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/fab29166f7141ca999988999b34e4012e28f0507.png"][/img][/p][p][b]RAAF Base Tindal / Katherine Tindal Airport (YPTN): [/b]Located to the east-southeast of the town of Katherine in Australia’s Northern Territory, this is a dual-use military (Royal Australian Air Force, RAAF) / civilian aerodrome.[/p][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/1b94f263946c9a53333dfef53e6e30f828030a85.png"][/img][/p][p][b]Lord Howe Island Airport (YLHI):[/b] This small airport provides air transportation to Lord Howe Island. Prior to its opening in September 1974, Lord Howe Island was served by flying boats. YLHI is an important transit and refueling point for light aircraft flying between Australia and New Zealand.[/p][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/001ff345a6e38afecf518db36f2ef1eaf58e5d17.png"][/img][/p][p][i]Microsoft Flight Simulator[/i] World Update 21: Australia features several incredible missions for both [i]Microsoft Flight Simulator[/i] (2020) and [i]Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024[/i].[/p][p][b][i]Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024[/i] World Update 21 Missions:[/b][/p][list][*][p][u]Three Discovery Flights[/u]: Canberra, Cocos Islands, and Whitsunday Islands[/p][/*][*][p][u]Five Precision Landings[/u]: Broome International Airport (YBRM) in a Boeing 737, Coober Pedy Airport in a CubCrafters XCub, RAAF Base Tindal / Katherine Tindal Airport (YPTN) in a Boeing F/A-18 Super Hornet, Mount Wedge helipad (Y904T) in a Robinson R66 helicopter, and South Solitary Island (YUXF1) in a Robinson 66 helcopter[/p][/*][*][p][u]Two Low Altitude Challenges[/u]: Lake Eildon in a Boeing F/A-18 Super Hornet and Kimbolton in a Aero Vodochody L-39 Albatros[/p][/*][*][p][u]Two Rally Race Challenges[/u]: Three Sisters and the Twelve Apostles[/p][/*][/list][p][b][i]Microsoft Flight Simulator[/i] (2020) World Update 21 Missions:[/b][/p][list][*][p][u]Three Discovery Flights[/u]: Canberra, Cocos Islands, and Whitsunday Islands[/p][/*][*][p][u]Three Landing Challenges[/u]: Broome International Airport (YBRM) in an Airbus A320, Coober Pedy Airport in a CubCrafters XCub, and RAAF Base Tindal / Katherine Tindal Airport (YPTN) in a Boeing F/A-18 Super Hornet[/p][/*][*][p][u]One Bush Trip[/u]: Australia’s Great Ocean Road[/p][/*][/list][p][i]Microsoft Flight Simulator[/i] World Update 21: Australia is FREE to all owners of [i]Microsoft Flight Simulator[/i]. Ensure that your simulator is updated to the latest version, download World Update 21, and explore the wonders of Australia. The sky is calling![/p][p][i]Microsoft Flight Simulator is available for Xbox Series X|S and PC with Xbox Game Pass, PC Game Pass, Windows, and Steam, PlayStation 5, and on Xbox One and supported mobile phones, tablets, and lower-spec PCs via Xbox Cloud Gaming. For the latest information on [u][url="https://www.xbox.com/en-US/games/microsoft-flight-simulator"]Microsoft Flight Simulator[/url][/u], stay tuned to [u][url="https://nam06.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Fmsfsofficial&data=04%7C01%7Csdamron%40microsoft.com%7Cf8bc9c21123c4a96cdad08d9638933b8%7C72f988bf86f141af91ab2d7cd011db47%7C1%7C0%7C637650263260978930%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C1000&sdata=JGnMisKNWgsAXVJ1sXZrD7xHUpe9RSqILuA7B2ochk0%3D&reserved=0"]@MSFSOfficial[/url][/u][/i][/p][p] on X (formerly known as Twitter) or visit our website at: [url="https://www.flightsimulator.com"]www.flightsimulator.com[/url].[/p][p][/p]

newsitems.title: City Update 13: California Now Available
newsitems.author: Jayneivana
newsitems.contents: [p][/p][previewyoutube="9bZ6U-V1u4A;full"][/previewyoutube][h3]Important note for MSFS (2020) users[/h3][p align="start"]You will need to manually update World Update II and World Update X from your in-sim Content Manager for City Update 13 to work correctly. [/p][p]Called the “Golden State” due to its Gold Rush of the mid-19th century, fields of golden poppies, and reputation for boundless prospects, California is renowned for its beauty and diversity. City Update 13 celebrates California in the Microsoft Flight Simulator World by adding breathtaking detail to 18 urban regions across this renowned state. The update includes some of the state’s most spectacular and notable locations, from its capital of Sacramento in the north, to San Diego at its south. Microsoft and partners created each of the 18 areas of interest (AOIs) using TIN (triangulated irregular network) surface texturing to provide Flight Simulator pilots with stunning vistas during flights over these remarkable cities.[/p][p]From north to south, City Update 13: California offers:[/p][h1][b]Sacramento[/b][/h1][p]Established in 1848 in the heart of California’s Great Central Valley, Sacramento is surrounded by broad swaths of verdant agriculture fields crisscrossed by canals and rivers. It is home to several notable sights, including the California State Capitol and Capitol Park, Tower Bridge, and The Ziggurat building.[/p][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/ebaaf4083a4f8963325400de205aa8e5d8d5e872.jpg"][/img][/p][h1][b]San Francisco Bay Area[/b][/h1][p]Cities in the update include San Rafael, Novato, Oakland, San Francisco, Fremont, and San Jose.[/p][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/4dcfef55a24839da614c1ae5cbbbf319e477c9b1.jpg"][/img]  [/p][p]Nestled between Mount Tamalpais (“Mount Tam”) and San Francisco Bay, [b]San Rafael[/b] is known for its beautiful architecture and natural setting in Marin County, part of the North Bay region of the San Francisco Bay Area.[/p][p]Situated on the northwestern shore of San Pablo Bay near the wine country regions of Sonoma and Napa Valleys, [b]Novato[/b] is renowned as a center of scientific research and biopharmaceutical innovation.[/p][p][b]Oakland[/b], located in the East Bay of the San Francisco Bay area, is home to Jack London Square, Lake Merritt, and the San Francisco – Oakland Bay Bridge.[/p][p][b]San Francisco[/b] is home to some of the world’s most famous landmarks, including the Transamerica Pyramid, the Golden Gate Bridge, Coit Tower, Alcatraz Island, and Fisherman’s Wharf.[/p][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/20804b618bfd53c414136012daf9f1cbfc7369d8.jpg"][/img]  [/p][p][b]Fremont[/b] is located on the southeastern shore of the San Francisco Bay and is known for the Old Mission San Jose, an important Spanish mission that was founded in 1797.[/p][p]A vital part of the Silicon Valley technology region, [b]San Jose [/b]is the most populous city in the South Bay region of the San Francisco Bay Area and the third most populous city in California. It is home to several renowned landmarks including San Jose City Hall, The 88, and Bank of Italy Building.[/p][h1][b]Key Cities of the San Joaquin Valley[/b][/h1][p]The San Joaquin Valley composes the southern portion of California’s Great Central Valley and is known for its agricultural opulence. The region includes the cities of Modesto, Fresno, and Visalia.[/p][p][b]Modesto[/b] is world renowned as the setting for the film American Graffiti and its proximity to Yosemite National Park. It is home to several notable landmarks, including the Modesto Arch, McHenry Mansion, and the Gallo Center for the Arts.[/p][p][b]Fresno[/b] (from the Spanish word for “ash tree”) is known as the heart of the San Joaquin Valley agriculture industry as Fresno County is consistently ranked the top agriculture producing county in the United States by economic output. Notable sights in Fresno include the Old Fresno Water Tower and the Kearney Mansion.[/p][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/2733901e47df4c6938066caa3bb2771f38498268.jpg"][/img]  [/p][p][b]Visalia[/b] is notable as the gateway to Sequoia and Kings Canyon National Parks, two renowned natural areas in the southern Sierra Nevada mountains. Landmarks in Visalia include the Palace Hotel and Fox Theater. [/p][h1][b]Cities of California’s Central Coast[/b][/h1][p]The update includes Santa Maria and Santa Barbara, both key cities in California’s Central Coast, an area known for its mediterranean climate, dramatic Pacific coastline, and beautiful vineyard-adorned hillsides.[/p][p][b]Santa Maria[/b] is known for its vineyards of cool-climate grapes, including Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, and Syrah, gorgeous coastal views, and Santa Maria-style barbecue (beef tri-tip slow cooked over coast live oak). Landmarks include the Santa Maria Museum of Flight and the La Purisima Mission.[/p][p][b]Santa Barbara’s[/b] stunning mountainous coastline, Mediterranean climate, and Spanish-style architecture have earned the area the nickname “The American Riviera.” Landmarks include Old Mission Santa Barbara, Stearns Wharf, and Santa Barbara Harbor.[/p][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/8e7d17f916a21f88a47985ca2c49b2cdaa624f32.jpg"][/img]  [/p][h1][b]Greater Los Angeles[/b][/h1][p]Greater Los Angeles is the most populous metropolitan area in California and the second most populous in the United States after the New York metropolitan area. The region is characterized by natural, cultural, economic, and architectural diversity, fascinating history, and beautiful landmarks. Cities highlighted include Santa Clarita, Glendale, Los Angeles, Inglewood, Long Beach, Anaheim, and Irvine.[/p][p]Located in northern Los Angeles County, [b]Santa Clarita[/b] is renowned as a location for numerous movies and television shows, earning it the nickname “Hollywood North.” Landmarks include Vasquez Rocks Natural Area, Six Flags Magic Mountain, and Melody Ranch Motion Picture Studio.[/p][p]Located just north of Downtown Los Angeles, [b]Glendale[/b] is known for its wide variety of retail shopping and animation studios. Notable locations include the Museum of Neon Art, Forest Lawn Memorial Park, and the Alex Theater.[/p][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/2afcf56c9bd0d0d429840f08bd68436b366ea238.jpg"][/img]  [/p][p][b]Los Angeles[/b], the second largest city by population in the United States, is home to numerous internationally acclaimed landmarks. Some of the most notable include Griffith Observatory, Walt Disney Concert Hall, U.S. Bank Tower, and Wilshire Grand Center.[/p][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/84c308bb2a03115c1f5557a3b5fe0dd76c407bf1.jpg"][/img]  [/p][p]Located adjacent to Los Angeles International Airport, [b]Inglewood[/b] hosts several major professional sporting and entertainment events each year at Kia Forum, SoFi Stadium, and Intuit Dome, all located within its city limits.[/p][p]Located on the Pacific Ocean’s San Pedro Bay, [b]Long Beach[/b] is a major economic hub due to the area’s petroleum industry and the Port of Long Beach (the second busiest in the United States, behind the Port of Los Angeles). Major sights in Long Beach include the ocean liner RMS Queen Mary, the Aquarium of the Pacific, and the Shoreline Gateway Tower.[/p][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/d53789e1dc95f96e036c9396f9db03b42a8b1692.jpg"][/img]  [/p][p]The [b]Anaheim[/b] region is home to Disneyland, one of the world’s most visited theme parks, and Knott’s Berry Farm, known for its roller coasters and multi-themed areas.[/p][p]Located in central Orange County, [b]Irvine[/b] is a major center for several industries, including real estate development and technology. It is home to the Irvine Spectrum Center and Irvine Ranch Natural Landmarks. [/p][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/7ba24ac6bd0ef976bd3ee2c1651f5cce5788727d.jpg"][/img]  [/p][h1][b]San Diego[/b][/h1][p]Boasting a stunning skyline and Mediterranean climate, San Diego is internationally recognized as one of the most beautiful cities in the world. The second most populous city in California, San Diego is a major center for several industries as well as the U.S. military. Notable landmarks include the USS Midway Museum, One America Plaza, and the Coronado Bridge.[/p][p][img src="{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/38414035/1468537b6403a0b3cb3eb9bfa6060a27bd18f13e.jpg"][/img]  [/p][p]City Update 13: California is available FREE to all owners of Microsoft Flight Simulator. Ensure that you have the latest simulator version installed ([b]1.39.9.0[/b] for MSFS2020 / [b]1.6.34.0[/b] for MSFS2024 / [b]1.6.34.0[/b] for MSFS2024 on Windows PC – Steam as of 01/28/2026), download City Update 13, and bask in the sights of the Golden State![/p][p][i]Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024 is available for Xbox Series X|S and PC with Xbox Game Pass, PC Game Pass, Windows, PlayStation 5, PlayStation 5 Pro, and Steam, and on Xbox One and supported mobile phones, tablets, and lower-spec PCs via Xbox Cloud Gaming. For the latest information on[url="https://www.xbox.com/en-US/games/microsoft-flight-simulator"][u] Microsoft Flight Simulator[/u][/url], stay tuned to[url="https://nam06.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Fmsfsofficial&data=04%7C01%7Csdamron%40microsoft.com%7Cf8bc9c21123c4a96cdad08d9638933b8%7C72f988bf86f141af91ab2d7cd011db47%7C1%7C0%7C637650263260978930%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C1000&sdata=JGnMisKNWgsAXVJ1sXZrD7xHUpe9RSqILuA7B2ochk0%3D&reserved=0"][u] @MSFSOfficial[/u][/url] on Twitter.[/i][/p]
